act-caitlin-tough-brindabella.summary: As ACT Labor candidate for Brindabella in the 2024 election, Tough provided a candidate statement to Canberrans for a Good Death adopting the ACT Labor statement, which expressed support for the ACT's voluntary assisted dying legislation and noted all ACT Labor MLAs voted in favour of it. (Stated position; she was elected in November 2024 and did not vote on the 2024 Act.)

sources.title: Appendix: Responses from Individual Candidates - Statement by Caitlin Tough, ACT Labor candidate for Brindabella

act-fiona-carrick-murrumbidgee.summary: In a candidate statement to Canberrans for a Good Death ahead of the 2024 election, Carrick wrote 'I support Voluntary Assisted Dying, including the current legislation,' adding any change to the Act's review timeframe should involve stakeholder and community consultation. (Stated position; she was elected in November 2024 and did not vote on the 2024 Act.)

act-thomas-emerson-kurrajong.summary: In response to the Canberrans for a Good Death 2024 election candidate survey, Emerson (Independents for Canberra, Kurrajong) answered 'Yes' to the question 'Do you support VAD', and supported widening eligibility and a binding advance directive in the next Assembly term. (Stated position; he was elected in November 2024 and did not vote on the 2024 Act.)

sources.title: Report on the survey of candidates for the 2024 ACT Election and their attitudes to widening eligibility for voluntary assisted dying
